# Groundwater Geochemistry

**Summary**: The study of the chemical composition and evolution of groundwater.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] focused on groundwater-geochemistry to determine major ion distribution in the aquifers.

## Key Subtopics

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] ['major ion distribution', 'hydrogeochemical facies', 'TDS-EC relationship']

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] ['hydrochemical_analysis', 'Piper trilinear plots']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Both aquifers are over-saturated with calcite and dolomite, and under-saturated with gypsum. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 1]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] Determining the impact of aquifer boundaries and mineral interaction on water chemistry.
